raw_result.description: Payments platform with APIs for charges, subscriptions, payouts, and related financial operations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Stripe documents API-key authentication using secret or restricted keys and supports sending the key as a Bearer token or via HTTP Basic auth with the key as username and an empty password; Connect also uses a Stripe-Account header for acting on connected accounts.
raw_result.gating_reason: Stripe provides public API documentation and standard self-serve account signup; the pricing page shows pay-as-you-go transaction pricing rather than a sales-gated API program for basic access.

api_surface.breadth: Broad REST API covering core payments plus customers, subscriptions, invoices, payouts, files, search, and Connect-related operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Stripe officially documents an MCP server with tools including stripe_api_read, stripe_api_write, stripe_api_search, and search_stripe_documentation.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Stripe documents a global rate limiter of 100 operations per second in live mode and 25 operations per second in sandbox, with narrower limits for some endpoints such as Search API, Files API, and Payouts; 429 Too Many Requests is used for rate-limit responses.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official client libraries are documented across major languages, the API base is publicly documented, and Stripe publishes first-party MCP docs for AI-agent usage.

result.description: Payments platform with APIs for charges, subscriptions, payouts, and related financial operations.
result.auth_notes: Stripe documents API-key authentication using secret or restricted keys and supports sending the key as a Bearer token or via HTTP Basic auth with the key as username and an empty password; Connect also uses a Stripe-Account header for acting on connected accounts.
result.gating_reason: Stripe provides public API documentation and standard self-serve account signup; the pricing page shows pay-as-you-go transaction pricing rather than a sales-gated API program for basic access.

api_surface.breadth: Broad REST API covering core payments plus customers, subscriptions, invoices, payouts, files, search, and Connect-related operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Stripe officially documents an MCP server with tools including stripe_api_read, stripe_api_write, stripe_api_search, and search_stripe_documentation.

result.rate_limit_notes: Stripe documents a global rate limiter of 100 operations per second in live mode and 25 operations per second in sandbox, with narrower limits for some endpoints such as Search API, Files API, and Payouts; 429 Too Many Requests is used for rate-limit responses.
result.notable_signals: Official client libraries are documented across major languages, the API base is publicly documented, and Stripe publishes first-party MCP docs for AI-agent usage.
